name: Static-patch holography for de Sitter
summary: An alternative to global dS/CFT in which a single observer's causal diamond ('static patch') in de Sitter space is dual to a quantum-mechanical (not field-theoretic) system with finite Hilbert space dimension dim(H) ~ exp(Area_horizon / 4 G_N). Recent concrete realization: the double-scaled SYK model (DSSYK, Lin-Stanford-Maldacena 2022) appears to reproduce the dS static-patch structure exactly. Provides a candidate non-perturbative formulation of quantum gravity in de Sitter without relying on global asymptotic boundary structure. Resolves observer-dependent QG issues but requires giving up familiar tools like S-matrix and asymptotic charges.

addresses_puzzles.note: Provides candidate non-perturbative QG-in-dS formulation without requiring global boundary structure. Replaces AdS-style asymptotic holography with finite-dim Hilbert space attached to observer's causal patch. Concrete DSSYK realization (arXiv:2206.14104) gives explicit dual.

Static-patch / cosmological horizon shares features with BH horizon (Bekenstein-Hawking entropy, Hawking temperature T = H/2pi); same techniques apply. Suggests cosmological observer recovers information about their past via thermal patch reconstruction analogous to interior reconstruction in BH islands.

new_scales: Hubble scale H ~ 10^-33 eV (sets dS temperature T = H/2pi)
introduces.note: Reformulation, not new physics: takes cosmological constant Lambda as input, predicts finite-dim Hilbert space dim(H) = exp(S_BH) for an observer's static patch.

history: Banks-Fischler (2001), 'M-theory observables for cosmological space-times', hep-th/0102077 - early finite-dim Hilbert space proposal. Static-patch revival: Susskind (2021), 'Three lectures on complexity and black holes', arXiv:1810.11563 generalized to dS. Concrete DSSYK realization: Lin (2022), 'The bulk Hilbert space of double scaled SYK', JHEP 11, 060, arXiv:2208.07032; Susskind (2022), arXiv:2209.09999. Active research direction 2022-2025.

references.citation: Lin, 'The bulk Hilbert space of double scaled SYK', JHEP 11, 060 (2022)
Banks and Fischler, 'M-theory observables for cosmological space-times', arXiv:hep-th/0102077 (2001)
